title: baileya multiradiata var. pleniradiata
intro: Baileya multiradiata var. pleniradiata, also known as Desert Marigold or Pleniradiata Baileya, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its bright yellow daisy-like flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Baileya multiradiata var. pleniradiata include treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as acting as a natural remedy for fever and gastrointestinal problems. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by drying the flowers and leaves and then using them in various forms to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
